mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate above 5.5% in 3 consecutive months signals a weakening labor market that reduces consumer spending and corporate profitability, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with rising unemployment rate are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A decline in industrial production below 95 for 2 consecutive months signals a weakening supply chain that reduces corporate profitability and investment, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in periods with declining industrial production are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in housing starts above 1.2 million units in 2 consecutive months signals an increase in consumer spending and corporate investment, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST > 1.2 million units for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with rising housing starts are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the federal funds rate above 4.5% in 3 consecutive months signals a tightening monetary cycle that increases discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in periods with rising federal funds rate are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A decline in initial claims below 200,000 in 2 consecutive weeks signals a strengthening labor market that reduces consumer spending and corporate profitability, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in periods with declining initial claims are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in real GDP above 3.5% in 2 consecutive quarters signals an increase in consumer spending and corporate investment, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with rising real GDP are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the real sales of manufacturers below 90 for 2 consecutive months signals a weakening supply chain that reduces corporate profitability and investment, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in periods with declining real sales of manufacturers are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).
